title: PARENT MENTOR PROGRAM - 211info
text: MORRISON CHILD AND FAMILY SERVICES SALEM PARENT MENTOR PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides peer mentoring to parents impacted by addiction who are involved in ODHS Child Welfare and criminal justice system. Parents are matched with a mentor who helps them stay on track, work through child welfare system and complete substance abuse treatment. Mentors help connect parents to recovery communities. The program is a part of Parents Anonymous Oregon. 3857 Wolverine Street NE Suite 22 Salem, OR 97305 Eligibility: Available to any parent who: -Has an open Child Welfare case due to substance abuse -Is currently in treatment for substance abuse -Is reuniting with their children after successful treatment Hours: Message line: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 258-4685 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 258-4685 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None required at intake LANGUAGES: English, Spanish AREA SERVED: State of Oregon Services: Parent Support Groups * Substance Use Disorder Issues Parent to Parent Networking * Substance Use Disorder Issues If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. MORRISON CHILD AND FAMILY SERVICES SALEM
